name: Lisas' Egg Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 package eggroll wrappers, in veggie department
1/2 cup green onions with tops, shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse bean sprouts and pat dry with paper towel.
2. Clean and finely shred bok choi cabbage.
3. Fry pork until it loses its pink color, then add bamboo shoots, followed by bean sprouts and cabbage. Cook for 1 minute.
4. Add green onions, sugar, salt, and soy sauce. Cook for another minute.
5. Blend 4 teaspoons of cornstarch with 4 teaspoons of water until smooth. Stir into the pan and cook for 1 minute or until thickened. Allow to cool before handling.
6. Place the filling in a colander and drain any excess liquid. Beat the egg with 1 tablespoon of water.
7. Lay an egg roll wrapper with one corner pointing toward you. Place about 2 tablespoons of filling just below the center of the wrapper.
8. Fold the bottom corner up, then fold in the sides (envelope style).
9. Brush the top edge of the triangle with the egg mixture, then roll up the rest to seal tightly. Ensure it is securely sealed with "egg glue."
10. Repeat with remaining wrappers.
11. Heat peanut oil to 360°F.
12. Fry 4-6 rolls at a time until browned on all sides, about 3 minutes.
13. Place fried rolls on an absorbent paper-lined pan in a warm oven to keep hot while cooking the remaining rolls.
14. Serve with sweet and sour or hot mustard.
